Title: National Hackathon Challenge
Description: National level hackathon focusing on solving real-world problems using technology and innovation.
Requirements: Team size: 2-4 members, Cross-department teams allowed, Original project ideas

Title: Intel Hardware Design Challenge
Description: Hardware design competition focusing on IoT and embedded systems innovation.
Requirements: Knowledge of embedded systems, Hardware design experience, Team size: 2-3 members

Title: Smart India Hackathon 2025
Department: CSE, IT, ECE, EEE, MECH, CIVIL
Description: National level hackathon to solve problems proposed by various government ministries and industries.
Requirements: Team size: 6 members, At least 2 female team members, One unique solution per team

Title: Mercuria Hackathon Singapore 2025
Eligibility: Students in CS, Data Science, or related fields
Description: A 48-hour in-person hackathon at Marina Bay Sands, Singapore, focusing on global shipping, digital security, and environmental sustainability.
Requirements: Individual applications only, Teams formed by organizers, Participation in person

Title: MEGA Hackathon 2025
Description: An online hackathon integrating computer science, STEM, economics, and infrastructure, aimed at advancing human development.
Requirements: Team size: Flexible, Open to high school and college students, Focus on sustainable development

Title: Global AI Hackathon 2025
Stipend: Trip to MIT AI & Education Summit
Description: A virtual hackathon hosted by MIT RAISE, encouraging participants to build AI apps aligned with the UN Sustainable Development Goals.
Requirements: Use of MIT App Inventor, Focus on social impact, Submission of AI-based applications

Title: NextStep Hacks 2025
Description: An online hackathon focusing on creating solutions that empower people with disabilities, promoting inclusivity and accessibility.
Requirements: Team size: Flexible, Projects aimed at assistive technologies, Participation in workshops and mentor sessions

Title: World Engineering Day Hackathon 2025
Eligibility: Engineering students and secondary school students worldwide
Description: A hackathon organized by UNESCO and WFEO, focusing on SDG 1: No Poverty, encouraging teams to create solutions for real-world scenarios.
Requirements: Team-based participation, Creation of a short video outlining the solution, Focus on poverty alleviation

Title: HackZurich 2025
Description: Europe's largest hackathon held in Zurich, Switzerland, bringing together over 600 participants to develop innovative prototypes over a weekend.
Requirements: Team size: Typically 2-5 members, On-site participation, Development of functional prototypes
